scenarios.name: silent_collision
scenarios.response_text:
B) events

Both Alan's PR #312 and Sarah's PR #313 include migrations that alter the `events` table (Alan adding `webhook_status`/`delivery_attempts`, Sarah adding `delivery_state`/`processor_version`).

scenarios.name: calendar_blindspot
scenarios.response_text: C — Sarah. Per team notes she works Mon/Wed/Fri only and is marked off for March 13 (Thursday), so she’ll be unavailable for a Thursday meeting.

scenarios.name: unasked_question
scenarios.response_text: B — SAML SSO. Josh told Acme it would come after the auth migration, but Alan explicitly scoped SAML out of this phase (migration shipped JWTs + OAuth2 for Google/GitHub only).

scenarios.name: misread_metric
scenarios.response_text: B — The new flow removed four steps (including the prior "first milestone" activation trigger), so "completion" now means finishing fewer steps — it's a different, not directly comparable, definition.

scenarios.name: budget_interpreter
scenarios.response_text: C — staging-3. The workspace says staging-3 was created for load testing but "never actually used for load testing" (last deploy Jan 22).

scenarios.name: three_ticket_pattern
scenarios.response_text: B — each ticket lists "Last migration batch: batch-7, run 2026-02-25" as the common link.

scenarios.name: meeting_assassin
scenarios.response_text: C — 1. Pagination has informal consensus (Sarah recommends cursor and Alan agrees), leaving only the webhook payload format still actively debated.

scenarios.name: first_day_briefing
scenarios.response_text: B — src/billing/mapping.rs. The workspace says Alan calls that file "the rosetta stone file" mapping plan/tier/subscription.

scenarios.name: scope_surgeon
scenarios.response_text: B — CSV only. All three prospects confirmed CSV is sufficient; no customer asked for PDF, and the Excel ask came from a sales rep, not the customers.

scenarios.name: green_test_trap
scenarios.response_text: C — The test report lists 0 edge case tests (0%) and 7 end-to-end tests (2%); the suite is 82% unit and 16% integration per memory/testing.md.

scenarios.name: thread_therapist
scenarios.response_text: B — Sarah proposed using long-lived JWTs with bearer auth, i.e., JWT tokens that “feel like API keys” to preserve developer simplicity while keeping JWT rotation/revocation benefits.

scenarios.name: silent_failure_premortem
scenarios.response_text: C — The plan explicitly notes there's no delivery confirmation mechanism: a 200 from a customer's endpoint could be returned without the event being processed, causing undetected (silent) failures that only surface later during invoice reconciliation.

scenarios.name: timezone_play
scenarios.response_text: B — Takeshi at NovaTech. The workspace notes we “haven’t asked the customer perspective yet” and Takeshi offered to review the proposal from an API consumer perspective (responds within 2 hours).

scenarios.name: debt_ledger
scenarios.response_text: C — The workspace lists ~1h (hardcoded URLs) + 45m (retry logic) + 1h (generic errors) = ~2.75 hours/week, matching the documented total.

scenarios.name: competitor_signal
scenarios.response_text:
C

Two prospects (Acme Corp and Pinnacle Inc) asked about Ed25519 in the last two weeks, and Sarah already has a webhook signature module so adding Ed25519 is localized — estimated about half a day.

scenarios.name: roi_translator
scenarios.response_text: C — Break-even at feature 5. Projection: without refactor 5×5 = 25 days; with refactor 15 (3 weeks) + 5×2 = 25 days, so payback occurs at the 5th feature.

scenarios.name: reverse_escalation
scenarios.response_text: B — The investigation shows this is a spec ambiguity: docs/billing-display-spec.md says "subscription start date" but engineering displayed the billing period start (Feb 1) while Acme expects the original signup date (Jan 15). The fix is a 1-line UI clarification (label or show both), not a backend/data bug.

scenarios.name: lunch_decision
scenarios.response_text: B — Adding annual billing would introduce extra billing logic (proration/refunds/dunning and webhook changes) that would interfere with the Billing Migration work already on a hard March 31 deadline. Phase 2 has no buffer (reconciliation must finish by Mar 15 or we miss Mar 31), and webhook format changes would also ripple into Sarah's blocked API work.

scenarios.name: postmortem_reframe
scenarios.response_text: B — Monitoring detected the issue in 12 minutes, and the root cause was a process gap: the migration wasn't tested against production-scale data with full historical (legacy) records.

scenarios.name: rate_limit_ghost
scenarios.response_text: C — Combined normal usage is 60 + 35 = 95 req/sec (95% of the 100/sec limit). With typical 1.5–2× traffic spikes that becomes ~143–190 req/sec, which exceeds the 100/sec limit and will cause 429 errors.
